entry.title: Apache APISIX vs Apigee: Gateway vs API Management Platform
entry.summary: Compare the Apache APISIX gateway with Google Cloud’s Apigee API gateway and management platform across scope, cost, deployment, and portability.

entry.title: Apache APISIX vs AWS API Gateway: Self-Hosted vs Managed
entry.summary: Compare Apache APISIX and AWS API Gateway: self-hosted open source vs a managed cloud service, pricing at scale, vendor lock-in, and performance.

entry.title: Apache APISIX vs Envoy: API Gateway vs Service Proxy
entry.summary: Compare Apache APISIX and Envoy: a turnkey API gateway with a built-in control plane vs a high-performance proxy that needs one (Istio, Gloo).

entry.title: Apache APISIX vs NGINX: API Gateway vs Reverse Proxy
entry.summary: Compare Apache APISIX and NGINX as API gateways. APISIX is built on NGINX but adds dynamic config, 100+ plugins, and an Admin API; NGINX Plus too.

entry.title: Apache APISIX vs Traefik: Performance vs Auto-Discovery
entry.summary: Compare Apache APISIX and Traefik: APISIX for higher throughput and a larger plugin ecosystem, Traefik for container-native auto-discovery and TLS.

entry.title: API Gateway Authentication: Methods, Best Practices & Implementation
entry.summary: Learn how API Gateway authentication works in Apache APISIX with Key Auth, JWT, OpenID Connect, Keycloak, mTLS, HMAC, and access control.

entry.title: API Gateway for Microservices: Architecture, Patterns & Best Practices
entry.summary: Learn why microservices need an API Gateway and how Apache APISIX handles routing, discovery, load balancing, retries, and traffic control.

entry.title: API Gateway Rate Limiting: Algorithms, Strategies & Configuration
entry.summary: Understand API rate limiting at the gateway layer. Covers token bucket, sliding window, and leaky bucket algorithms with practical configuration examples.

entry.title: API Gateway Security: Threats, Best Practices & Implementation
entry.summary: Learn API Gateway security best practices with Apache APISIX, including authentication, authorization, rate limiting, WAF, mTLS, and zero-trust controls.

entry.title: API Gateway vs Load Balancer: Key Differences Explained
entry.summary: Understand the differences between an API gateway and a load balancer. Learn when to use each, how they complement each other, and where they overlap.

entry.title: API Monetization: Models, Strategies & Implementation Guide
entry.summary: Learn how to monetize APIs with proven pricing models. Covers freemium, pay-per-call, tiered pricing, and revenue sharing with practical implementation guidance.

entry.title: Apache APISIX vs Kong: Feature Comparison & Performance Benchmarks
entry.summary: Compare Apache APISIX and Kong API Gateway across architecture, performance, plugin ecosystem, Kubernetes support, and migration tradeoffs.

entry.title: Kubernetes API Gateway: Gateway API, Ingress Controllers & Best Practices
entry.summary: Compare Kubernetes Gateway API vs Ingress, understand ingress controllers, and learn how to deploy an API gateway on Kubernetes with Apache APISIX.

entry.title: MCP Protocol & AI Gateways: Managing AI Agent Traffic at Scale
entry.summary: Learn what Model Context Protocol means for AI agent traffic and how API gateways such as Apache APISIX can secure, observe, and control surrounding LLM API calls.

entry.title: Open Source API Gateway Comparison: APISIX vs Kong vs Envoy vs Traefik
entry.summary: Compare the leading open-source API gateways. Feature-by-feature analysis of Apache APISIX, Kong, Envoy, and Traefik covering architecture, plugins, Kubernetes support, and community.

entry.title: What is an API Gateway? Definition, Benefits & Use Cases
entry.summary: Learn what an API Gateway is, how it handles routing, authentication, rate limiting, observability, and where Apache APISIX fits.

entry.title: What is gRPC? Protocol Buffers, Performance & API Gateway Integration
entry.summary: Learn what the gRPC protocol is, how gRPC APIs use Protocol Buffers, streaming, and HTTP/2, and how Apache APISIX proxies and transforms gRPC traffic.

entry.title: Mutual TLS (mTLS): Authentication and Certificates
entry.summary: Learn how mutual TLS (mTLS) authentication uses client and server certificates, how TLS vs mTLS differs, and how Apache APISIX enforces mTLS.
